prompt: The method is supposed to swap two elements in an array, but the array is unchanged after the call. What is the bug?
choices: The temp variable is declared inside the method and goes out of scope
Java passes primitives by value — swap receives copies of x and y; changes to a and b do not affect the originals
The method needs to be declared static to modify variables
int cannot be swapped; you must use Integer objects
explanation: Java is strictly pass-by-value for primitive types. swap() receives copies of x and y; reassigning a and b modifies only the local copies, leaving x and y in main unchanged. Fix: swap elements by index in an array (pass int[] arr, int i, int j) or return both values another way.
